_comment: Node 7 weight pins (locked decision #10 v1; Phase 2 v2 locked decisions #11, #12, #13). Every weight referenced in workflow.json / workflow_lineart_fallback.json / workflow_flux_v2.json MUST appear here so runpod_setup.sh can curl + sha256-verify before ComfyUI boots. A None/empty sha256 is a TODO marker -- operator fills in after the first known-good download on the pod. ComfyUI-Manager is the convenience path for local workflow authoring; the pod uses this file as the source of truth. Phase 1 weights (deprecated:true) are kept for the 6-month rollback window per locked decision #12; runpod_setup.sh skips them by default and downloads only when DOWNLOAD_DEPRECATED=true. Phase 2 weights split fp16 (default, A100 80GB) and fp8 (4090 24GB fallback) variants of the Flux UNET + T5-XXL text encoder; CLIP-L, Flux VAE, style LoRA, ControlNet Union Pro are precision-agnostic. The XLabs Flux IP-Adapter v2 weight + x-flux-comfyui custom node ship in Phase 2b, not 2a. Phase 2-revision (2026-04-28): the generic Flat Cartoon Style v1.2 LoRA stays pinned but its effective strength drops to 0.0 (orchestrate.py STYLE_LORA_STRENGTHS table) because it biases toward color, conflicting with Part 1's BnW line-art deliverable; the LoRA still loads (workflow can't skip the LoraLoader without rewiring) but contributes nothing. The Phase 2d-run-trained TMKOC v1 LoRA (placeholder entry below until that runs) stays at the locked-decision-#2 strength of 0.75 — it'll be a TMKOC-specific LINE-ART style LoRA trained on storyboard scene cuts, not colored TMKOC stills.

models.name: anyloraCheckpoint_bakedvaeBlessedFp16
models.role: SD 1.5 line-art checkpoint (Phase 1 base model for Node 7 txt2img).
models.deprecatedReason: Phase 2 Flux migration replaces SD 1.5 + AnyLoRA with Flux Kontext Dev fp16 + Flat Cartoon Style v1.2 LoRA. Real-shot TMKOC test exposed AnyLoRA produces anime-girl output instead of TMKOC characters because AnyLoRA is anime-trained.

models.name: bnw_lineart_v1
models.url: TODO: replace with a stable BnW / monochrome LoRA URL (civitai or huggingface)
models.role: Phase 1 BnW / monochrome LoRA stacked on AnyLoRA to bias output toward clean BnW line art.
models.deprecatedReason: Phase 2 replaces with the Phase 2d-run-trained TMKOC v1 line-art LoRA. (Earlier deprecation note claimed Phase 2 outputs colored TMKOC-style frames — Phase 2-revision on 2026-04-28 corrected that: Part 1's deliverable IS BnW line art, and v2's prompts + Phase 2d-run LoRA bias toward BnW just like Phase 1 did. The Flat Cartoon Style v1.2 LoRA shipped in Phase 2a colored output but is now bypassed via strength=0.0 in Phase 2-revision until the custom TMKOC line-art LoRA replaces it.) Kept deprecated because the Phase 2 path uses prompts + a future TMKOC-specific LoRA, not this generic SD 1.5 BnW LoRA.

models.name: control_v11p_sd15_openpose
models.role: Phase 1 OpenPose/DWPose ControlNet for the dwpose workflow (human characters).
models.deprecatedReason: Phase 2 replaces 3 separate SD 1.5 ControlNets (lineart + scribble + openpose) with a single Flux ControlNet Union Pro that switches modes via SetUnionControlNetType.

models.name: control_v11p_sd15_lineart
models.role: Phase 1 LineArt ControlNet for the lineart-fallback workflow (non-human characters).
models.deprecatedReason: Phase 2 replaces 3 separate SD 1.5 ControlNets with Flux ControlNet Union Pro.

models.name: control_v11p_sd15_scribble
models.role: Phase 1 Scribble ControlNet for the lineart-fallback workflow (paired with LineArt at 0.6 each).
models.deprecatedReason: Phase 2 replaces 3 separate SD 1.5 ControlNets with Flux ControlNet Union Pro.

models.name: ip-adapter-plus_sd15
models.role: Phase 1 IP-Adapter Plus SD1.5 - feeds the reference color crop to bias identity.
models.deprecatedReason: Phase 2b replaces with XLabs Flux IP-Adapter v2 (native Flux IP-Adapter). The SD 1.5 IP-Adapter Plus fork's weight_type parameter shape doesn't apply to the Flux variant.

models.name: clip-vision-vit-h
models.role: Phase 1 CLIP Vision encoder required by IP-Adapter Plus SD1.5.
models.deprecatedReason: Phase 2b's XLabs Flux IP-Adapter v2 uses Flux's own DualCLIPLoader (T5-XXL + CLIP-L) for embeddings; this CLIP-ViT-H weight is no longer referenced.

models.name: dwpose-yolox-l
models.role: DWPose body-detector (YOLOX-L). REUSED across Phase 1 + Phase 2 (DWPose preprocessor is the same in both workflows).

models.name: dwpose-dw-ll-ucoco-384
models.role: DWPose keypoint regressor (ll_ucoco_384). REUSED across Phase 1 + Phase 2.

models.name: flux1-dev-fp16
models.role: Phase 2 Flux Dev fp16 base model. Default for A100 80GB. Locked decision #1 (Flux Kontext Dev variant) + #11 (fp16 default).

models.name: flux1-dev-fp8
models.role: Phase 2 Flux Dev fp8 fallback for 4090-class GPUs (24GB VRAM). Locked decision #11 -- selected via --precision fp8.

models.name: t5xxl-fp16
models.role: Phase 2 T5-XXL text encoder fp16. Paired with flux1-dev-fp16 via DualCLIPLoader.

models.name: t5xxl-fp8
models.role: Phase 2 T5-XXL text encoder fp8 fallback. Paired with flux1-dev-fp8 via DualCLIPLoader.

models.name: clip-l
models.role: Phase 2 CLIP-L secondary text encoder. Precision-agnostic; loaded by DualCLIPLoader at both fp16 and fp8 paths.

models.name: flux-vae
models.role: Phase 2 Flux VAE (autoencoder). Mirrors the BFL Flux ae.safetensors via ffxvs (the BFL HF repo gates downloads).

models.name: flat-cartoon-style-v12
models.role: Phase 2 generic flat-cartoon style LoRA (Civitai 644541 v 740091). Phase 2-revision (2026-04-28): effective strength is 0.0 — the orchestrate.py STYLE_LORA_STRENGTHS table maps flat_cartoon_v12 → 0.0 (LoRA loads but is bypassed because it biases toward color, conflicting with Part 1's BnW deliverable). The locked-decision-#2 production strength of 0.75 still applies to the custom TMKOC v1 LoRA shipping in Phase 2d-run. The default --style-lora is still flat_cartoon_v12 because the LoRA file is a known-downloadable placeholder until tmkoc_v1 ships; once the TMKOC line-art LoRA exists, operators flip --style-lora=tmkoc_v1 to pick up the locked production strength.
models._civitai_note: Civitai requires authentication for direct API downloads; runpod_setup.sh prints a TODO message and skips the download if the URL returns a login redirect. Operator manually downloads via browser and scp's to the pod, then re-runs the setup.

models.name: tmkoc-style-v1
models.url: TODO: replace after the first known-good Phase 2d-run training lands a custom-trained tmkoc_style_v1.safetensors. The runbook at tools/phase2d/PHASE2D_TRAINING_PLAYBOOK.md walks through the training procedure. Phase 2-revision (2026-04-28) revised the playbook: training data is the user's storyboard scene cuts (clean digital BnW line art on white, no fill — the same target style v2 generates) rather than colored TMKOC final-rendered stills. Until the URL is filled in, runpod_setup.sh skips this entry with a TODO message. Hosting target: a private HuggingFace repo OR a tarball hosted on the user's own S3/CDN.
models.role: Phase 2d-run custom-trained TMKOC LINE-ART style LoRA. Replaces the generic Flat Cartoon Style v1.2 (--style-lora=flat_cartoon_v12, currently bypassed at strength 0.0 because it biases toward color) with a TMKOC-specific BnW LINE-ART style picked up by the v2 workflow_flux_v2.json node 20 LoraLoader when --style-lora=tmkoc_v1. Strength locked at 0.75 (locked decision #2; orchestrate.py STYLE_LORA_STRENGTHS['tmkoc_v1']). Trained on storyboard scene cuts (clean digital BnW lines, no fill) so it reinforces the line aesthetic v2's prompts already ask for. Until a real weight ships, --style-lora=tmkoc_v1 will fail at ComfyUI submission with a 'missing file' error from the LoraLoader; the placeholder entry here documents the contract so the runbook + tests can refer to it.
models._phase2d_note: Placeholder entry shipped as part of Phase 2d-prep (2026-04-27); revised in Phase 2-revision (2026-04-28) from 'TMKOC style LoRA' to 'TMKOC LINE-ART style LoRA'. The actual training run is a separate live-pod session (Phase 2d-run); see tools/phase2d/PHASE2D_TRAINING_PLAYBOOK.md for the procedure. After training ships, fill in `url` (HuggingFace or operator-hosted) + `sha256` (verified after first known-good download).

models.name: flux-controlnet-union-pro
models.role: Phase 2 ControlNet Union Pro (Shakker-Labs). Single CN model that switches between openpose / lineart / canny / depth via SetUnionControlNetType. Replaces the 3 separate Phase 1 SD 1.5 CN models. Strength 0.65 default per locked decision #6.

models.name: flux-ip-adapter-v2
models.role: Phase 2b XLabs Flux IP-Adapter v2 (https://huggingface.co/XLabs-AI/flux-ip-adapter-v2). Loaded by the LoadFluxIPAdapter node (internal class_type from x-flux-comfyui/nodes.py NODE_CLASS_MAPPINGS — no spaces, no typo). The display-name 'Load Flux IPAdatpter' shown in ComfyUI's GUI menu IS typo'd, but workflow JSON uses class_type, not display name. Locked decision #4 (Phase 2). ip_scale = 0.8 default per locked decision #6. Destination MUST be models/xlabs/ipadapters/ — that's where x-flux-comfyui's folder_paths registration looks (`folder_paths.folder_names_and_paths['xlabs_ipadapters'] = ([dir_xlabs_ipadapters], ...)` where `dir_xlabs_ipadapters = os.path.join(folder_paths.models_dir, 'xlabs', 'ipadapters')`). Phase 2d-fixup (2026-04-27) corrected this from a wrong earlier 'models/ipadapter-flux/' path that LoadFluxIPAdapter never scanned.

models.name: clip-vit-large-patch14
models.role: Phase 2b CLIP-L vision encoder (OpenAI's clip-vit-large-patch14). Required by XLabs Flux IP-Adapter v2 — the loader internally encodes the reference image with this model. Distinct from the Phase 2a clip-l text encoder (under models/text_encoders/), which is loaded by DualCLIPLoader for prompt embedding.

customNodes.name: comfyui_controlnet_aux
customNodes.role: Provides DWPreprocessor, LineArtPreprocessor, ScribblePreprocessor used by Node 7 workflows (Phase 1 dwpose + lineart-fallback; Phase 2 v2 reuses DWPreprocessor + LineArtPreprocessor).

customNodes.name: ComfyUI_IPAdapter_plus
customNodes.role: Phase 1: provides IPAdapterUnifiedLoader + IPAdapter nodes used by both Phase 1 workflows. Phase 2 deprecates this path -- v2 uses XLabs Flux IP-Adapter v2 via the x-flux-comfyui custom node (added in Phase 2b).
customNodes.deprecatedReason: Phase 2b's XLabs Flux IP-Adapter v2 uses the x-flux-comfyui custom node, not this SD 1.5 fork.

customNodes.name: x-flux-comfyui
customNodes.role: Phase 2b: provides LoadFluxIPAdapter + ApplyFluxIPAdapter (+ ApplyAdvancedFluxIPAdapter variant) classes used by workflow_flux_v2.json's nodes 22 + 24. Class names are the INTERNAL class_type strings from NODE_CLASS_MAPPINGS — no spaces, no typo. The 'IPAdatpter' typo is in upstream's NODE_DISPLAY_NAME_MAPPINGS (the GUI menu label) and does not appear in class_type. The input field name 'ipadatper' on LoadFluxIPAdapter IS typo'd verbatim (kept as-is in workflow JSON since it's the registered field name). Pairs with flux-ip-adapter-v2.safetensors weight pinned above (in models/xlabs/ipadapters/ where x-flux-comfyui's folder_paths registration looks). Required by Phase 2b's identity-injection layer.
